No immediate follow up actions until Mumtalakat/Wheelson confirm the way forward. 18th Mar - Final overview demo focussing on Sourcing, with Sourcing SMEs in attendance, next steps: Wheelson to prepare a detailed SWAT-based comparative summary of all 5 vendors for the client’s two‑day workshop in the second week of April, aiming to narrow from 5 to 2 vendors. Reach out to us over email with sharp / specific follow‑up questions or requests for deeper dives in particular areas, if needed before the April workshop. If Zycus is shortlisted into the next stage, come back to us for support on a more detailed evaluation, including a potential pilot/POC for one entity. Zycus provide additional clarifications, demos, or artefacts (e.g., screenshots like AI scoring, bid normalization details) whenever requested by Samynathan to support their internal analysis and April workshop. Be on standby for a deeper workshop or paid POC if Zycus is selected into the final 2–3 vendors in the long‑term evaluation phase 16th Mar - Initial overview of Zycus I2O (excluding CLM - out of scope), plus live demo, sourcing drill-down to follow as next step - scheduled for 18th Mar 9th Mar - Intro overview call with CEO ..... client is confidential, but is a similar description to the Bahraini Sovereign Wealth fund: Mumtalakat Holding Company . Situation • Wheelson (consultancy / solutions integrator) is advising a Bahrain-based investment company (c. $20bn AUM, 60 entities) on selecting a comprehensive, end-to-end digital procurement platform. • They currently use highly customized, point AI/GenAI solutions across multiple business areas (incl. procurement), which are hard to replicate or scale across customers. • For this client, they started with point solutions for tender management, supplier intelligence, and P2P automation for some mid-scale entities; engagement has deepened to an overall consulting mandate. Impact / Pain • Existing work led to fragmented, non‑repeatable solutions and difficulty scaling products or reusing them across entities. • Manufacturing/other sectors were slow to adopt AI; they’re looking for a domain where AI value is adopted more aggressively – hence pivot to procurement. • The client has 12+ years of supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence and wants an “intelligent compilation” of internal + external data for supplier choice and relationship management. • Procurement is treated as strategic, centrally governed from HQ across 60 entities, with hybrid central/decentral models, making governance, intake experience, and guided buying critical. (Proposed) Resolution • Wheelson is running a structured evaluation of “high edge” enterprise S2P suites; Zycus and Jaggaer are the final two contenders to complete initial evaluation. • Plan is: initial generic capabilities evaluation now (no NDA), then for 2 shortlisted vendors a deeper NDA-based workshop paid POC/POV for one entity, then scale to multiple entities if successful. • Next step for Zycus specifically: show end-to-end “demand to PO” process (excluding CLM), strong intake & guided buying, supplier intelligence , sourcing, and ability to build /extend AI agents (Merlin, orchestration, etc.) without heavy OEM dependence.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well articulated but never quantified. Per the deal log, Mumtalakat has 12+ years of internal supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence, fragmented point solutions across ~60 entities, and no comprehensive end-to-end digital S2P platform with structured intake and guided buying (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar 2026; MEDDPICC 2.0 Identify Pain). No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings figure has been stated, and no ROI model exists. Quantifying the value of supplier intelligence and intake automation for the pilot entity is the open move.

champion.narrative: Samynathan R., Co-Founder and CEO of Wheelson Technologies, is the primary and only champion: he arranged the intro call, had done prior research on Merlin, controls the evaluation, and has worked with Mumtalakat across multiple engagements (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 Champion). He is an active partner-side advocate who authored the evaluation structure — he is the PATH to power, not a bypass. The gap is that he is a consultancy champion, not an internal Mumtalakat champion. Build a parallel direct relationship at Mumtalakat THROUGH him so the deal is not blind if his influence wanes.

competition.narrative: Zycus is shortlisted into the enterprise track in a two-vendor finalist field. The early evaluation named Zycus and JAGGAER as the final two contenders, with Coupa, iValua, GEP and SAP Ariba also evaluated. The 15 Apr update names Aerchain alongside Zycus for the enterprise track and Zip for the small track (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-04-15). JAGGAER is the strongest current capabilities peer; the wedge is Merlin agentic capability, external supplier intelligence (D&B plus AI crawlers) and CLM integration, none yet validated in the NDA workshop.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly identified, owned by Mumtalakat and surfaced via Wheelson: no digitised end-to-end procurement platform across a hybrid/centralised model spanning ~60 entities; no external supplier intelligence despite 12 years of internal data; no structured intake and guided buying; and supplier relationship model gaps (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 Identify Pain; SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-03-09). The strategic driver is a pivot to procurement as the domain where AI value can be adopted aggressively. Pain is confirmed and aligned to Zycus strengths; it is the partner's read of the client, not yet validated directly with Mumtalakat.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown. The only structure logged is a planned NDA ahead of the deeper workshop and a paid POC/POV for one entity before scaling (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-03-09). No MSA, SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec process, or partner commercial terms between Zycus and Wheelson are documented. As a sovereign wealth fund the approval and procurement path likely runs through an investment committee, but it is unmapped. Confirming the paid-POC scope and NDA sequence in writing — and naming who owns it on the Zycus side — would create the first paper-process visibility.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Mumtalakat budget owner is identified by name. MEDDPICC 2.0 records the budget owner as 'Mumtalakat' generically and explicitly states the economic buyer is TBC, with the Wheelson CEO as the key contact representing the consultancy, not the end client (source: MEDDPICC 2.0). All engagement flows through Wheelson; Zycus has never met the prospect directly (source: SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-30). As a sovereign wealth fund, a paid POC and 60-entity rollout likely require investment-committee or board approval whose chain and thresholds are unknown. The path to power runs THROUGH the champion — arming Samynathan to broker the Mumtalakat sponsor is the top move, not cold-outreach into the end client.

decision_process.narrative: A documented two-stage motion: Wheelson runs vendor evaluation (overview demos 16 and 18 Mar, then a two-day client workshop in the second week of April to narrow five vendors to two), after which Mumtalakat engages directly for a deeper NDA-based workshop and a paid POC for one pilot entity before any group rollout (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 Decision Process; SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-03-18, 2026-04-15). Zycus was shortlisted into the enterprise track. The risk is that stage two (direct Mumtalakat engagement) has not started due to the war-driven delay, so the process is paused short of the buyer-facing milestone. The open move is to agree the restart trigger with the champion and pre-stage the workshop, not to chase a status update while the buyer is correctly heads-down.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are clearly documented: (1) intake management and guided buying as top priority, (2) supplier relationship management, (3) external supplier intelligence as the major sought gap and differentiator, (4) self-serve agentic capability to build agents without OEM dependency, and (5) CLM integration rather than replacement (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 Decision Criteria). These map directly to the Zycus next-step ask (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-03-09). The criteria are confirmed; what is unvalidated is Zycus's differentiation against JAGGAER/Aerchain, pending the NDA workshop.

15th April - Caught up with CEO of Wheelson for project update: Details are:

  • Confirmed the client is Mumtalaket - the Bahrani Sovereign Wealth Fund
  • Zycus has been shortlisted for phase 2 alongside Aerchain for the 'Enterprise' project track (and Zip for the 'Small' project track)
  • The war has caused some delay in the project, but expecting confirmation and clarification of next steps by mid May
  • Anticipated next step(s) will be a 1-2 day workshop in Bahrain demoing to stakeholders in the pilot organization
  • Initial plan was a paid proof of concept project with an enterprise pilot - prior to a roll-out across the group, not clear at this stage if such a roll-out will be mandated or optional - there are c60 entities of various sizes/shareholding - including: Gulf Air, Aluminum Bahrain, Batelco Group, McLaren Group, Nat Bank of Bahrain.
  • No immediate follow up actions until Mumtalakat/Wheelson confirm the way forward.

18th Mar - Final overview demo focussing on Sourcing, with Sourcing SMEs in attendance, next steps:

  • Wheelson to prepare a detailed SWAT-based comparative summary of all 5 vendors for the client’s two‑day workshop in the second week of April, aiming to narrow from 5 to 2 vendors. 
  • Reach out to us over email with sharp / specific follow‑up questions or requests for deeper dives in particular areas, if needed before the April workshop. 
  • If Zycus is shortlisted into the next stage, come back to us for support on a more detailed evaluation, including a potential pilot/POC for one entity. 
  • Zycus provide additional clarifications, demos, or artefacts (e.g., screenshots like AI scoring, bid normalization details) whenever requested by Samynathan to support their internal analysis and April workshop. 
  • Be on standby for a deeper workshop or paid POC if Zycus is selected into the final 2–3 vendors in the long‑term evaluation phase

16th Mar - Initial overview of Zycus I2O (excluding CLM - out of scope), plus live demo, sourcing drill-down to follow as next step - scheduled for 18th Mar

9th Mar - Intro overview call with CEO ..... client is confidential, but is a similar description to the Bahraini Sovereign Wealth fund: Mumtalakat Holding Company.

Situation

• Wheelson (consultancy / solutions integrator) is advising a Bahrain-based investment company (c. $20bn AUM, 60 entities) on selecting a comprehensive, end-to-end digital procurement platform. 

• They currently use highly customized, point AI/GenAI solutions across multiple business areas (incl. procurement), which are hard to replicate or scale across customers. 

• For this client, they started with point solutions for tender management, supplier intelligence, and P2P automation for some mid-scale entities; engagement has deepened to an overall consulting mandate. 

Impact / Pain

• Existing work led to fragmented, non‑repeatable solutions and difficulty scaling products or reusing them across entities. 

• Manufacturing/other sectors were slow to adopt AI; they’re looking for a domain where AI value is adopted more aggressively – hence pivot to procurement. 

• The client has 12+ years of supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence and wants an “intelligent compilation” of internal + external data for supplier choice and relationship management. 

• Procurement is treated as strategic, centrally governed from HQ across 60 entities, with hybrid central/decentral models, making governance, intake experience, and guided buying critical. 

(Proposed) Resolution

• Wheelson is running a structured evaluation of “high edge” enterprise S2P suites; Zycus and Jaggaer are the final two contenders to complete initial evaluation. 

• Plan is:

  •  
    •  
      • initial generic capabilities evaluation now (no NDA), then
      • for 2 shortlisted vendors a deeper NDA-based workshop
      • paid POC/POV for one entity, then scale to multiple entities if successful. 

• Next step for Zycus specifically: show end-to-end “demand to PO” process (excluding CLM), strong intake & guided buying, supplier intelligence, sourcing, and ability to build/extend AI agents (Merlin, orchestration, etc.) without heavy OEM dependence.

deltas.subject: Win strategy: make Merlin + supplier intelligence the centerpiece. The SWAT shortlist to 2 (Zycus + JAGGAER) means capabilities are close; the wedge is supplier intelligence (iSupplier + iRisk + ANA internal+external data compilation, which JAGGAER is weak on) and Merlin AI extensibility (build/orchestrate agents without OEM lock-in, directly addressing their fragmented-custom-AI pain). The NDA workshop is the moment to demonstrate the Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history and to show a live supplier-intelligence dashboard mashing internal Mumtalakat data with external signals. If sourcing and intake were table stakes in the March demos, Merlin and intelligence are the differentiators for the final decision.

value.flag: Win strategy: make Merlin + supplier intelligence the centerpiece. The SWAT shortlist to 2 (Zycus + JAGGAER) means capabilities are close; the wedge is supplier intelligence (iSupplier + iRisk + ANA internal+external data compilation, which JAGGAER is weak on) and Merlin AI extensibility (build/orchestrate agents without OEM lock-in, directly addressing their fragmented-custom-AI pain). The NDA workshop is the moment to demonstrate the Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history and to show a live supplier-intelligence dashboard mashing internal Mumtalakat data with external signals. If sourcing and intake were table stakes in the March demos, Merlin and intelligence are the differentiators for the final decision.

Situation

• Wheelson (consultancy / solutions integrator) is advising a Bahrain-based investment company (c. $20bn AUM, 60 entities) on selecting a comprehensive, end-to-end digital procurement platform.\xa0

• They currently use highly customized, point AI/GenAI solutions across multiple business areas (incl. procurement), which are hard to replicate or scale across customers.\xa0

• For this client, they started with point solutions for tender management, supplier intelligence, and P2P automation for some mid-scale entities; engagement has deepened to an overall consulting mandate.\xa0

Impact / Pain

• Existing work led to fragmented, non‑repeatable solutions and difficulty scaling products or reusing them across entities.\xa0

• Manufacturing/other sectors were slow to adopt AI; they’re looking for a domain where AI value is adopted more aggressively – hence pivot to procurement.\xa0

• The client has 12+ years of supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence and wants an “intelligent compilation” of internal + external data for supplier choice and relationship management.\xa0

• Procurement is treated as strategic, centrally governed from HQ across 60 entities, with hybrid central/decentral models, making governance, intake experience, and guided buying critical.\xa0

(Proposed) Resolution

• Wheelson is running a structured evaluation of “high edge” enterprise S2P suites; Zycus and Jaggaer are the final two contenders to complete initial evaluation.\xa0

• Plan is:

  • \xa0
    • \xa0
      • initial generic capabilities evaluation now (no NDA), then
      • for 2 shortlisted vendors a deeper NDA-based workshop
      • paid POC/POV for one entity, then scale to multiple entities if successful.\xa0

• Next step for Zycus specifically: show end-to-end “demand to PO” process (excluding CLM), strong intake & guided buying, supplier intelligence, sourcing, and ability to build/extend AI agents (Merlin, orchestration, etc.) without heavy OEM dependence.\xa0
